content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 5, 1987


Mr. Cortus T. Koehler, Vice President
Board of Directors
Sacramento Municipal Utility District
P.O. Box 15830
Sacramento, CA  95852-1830

                                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                                         Our File No. I-87-122

Dear Mr. Koehler:

     I have been assigned your letter of April 16, 1987,
regarding the Arthur Anderson & Co. review of SMUD's contract
with Management Analysis Corporation.  As I understand it, that
contract involved the services of John Ward and his role at
Rancho Seco. In light of the developments reported in the media
last week, do you still need this agency's guidance? Please
advise.

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    [illegible]
                                                    Robert E. Leidigh
                                                    Counsel, Legal Division

REL;km




428 I Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

SMUD
SACRAMENTO MUNICIPAL UTILITY DISTRICT ■ P. O. Box 15830, Sacramento CA 95852-1830, (916) 452-3211
AN ELECTRIC SYSTEM SERVING THE HEART OF CALIFORNIA

April 16, 1987


Diane Griffiths, Esq.
General Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

There has been some discussion in the media about this
District's contract with Management Analysis Corporation
(MAC). We are obtaining the services of our Deputy General
Manager, Nuclear, and several other top Rancho Seco managers
under that contract.

I would like you to know that the District is about to ask
Arthur Andersen & Co., its auditor, to review the manner in
which the District's contract with MAC has been administered so
that we can be sure that the administration of that contract
has been in conformity with the Political Reform Act of 1974.
Arthur Andersen & Co. will be directed to consult your office
for guidance on the requirements of the Act if any legal
questions arise so that everyone concerned, including your
agency, will know that the audit is completely independent.

I am writing this letter so that you will have a clear, direct
statement from a SMUD Board member as to why this action is
being taken. The purpose of the audit is to give everyone in
the community assurance that the administration of the MAC
contract has received an independent, outside review.

Sincerely,

Cortus T. Koehler
Vice-President
Board of Directors


DISTRICT HEADQUARTERS ■ 6201 S Street, Sacramento CA 95817-1899

April 21, 1987




Cortus T. Koehler
Vice-President
Board of Directors
Sacramento Municipal Utility District
P.O. Box 15830
Sacramento, CA  95852-1830

Re:  87-122

Dear Mr. Koehler:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on April 20, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert E. Leidigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If your request is for informal assistance, we will
answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).)  You also should be aware
that your letter and our response are public records which may
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

Sacramento, CA
(Sacramento Co.)
Docket
(Cir.D.219,057)
(Cir.S.252,212)

APR 7 - 1987

Allen's P.C.B. Ent. 1988

SMUD set for effort to hire its top nuclear adviser

By Doug Dempster
Bee Staff Writer

SMUD directors are expected to
begin negotiating as early as this
week to place their nuclear chief,
John Ward, directly on the utility
payroll.

Ward, in charge of efforts to return the Rancho Seco nuclear plant
to service, carries the SMUD title of
deputy general manager but actually
works for a consulting firm, Management Analysis Co., which was hired
by SMUD to manage the re-start effort.

Director Ed Smeloff said Monday
that the full board and Ward agreed

in a closed session last week to start
talking.

Ward said he would be "proud to
be associated with SMUD," but added that he did not know if the utility
could make it financially attractive
enough for him to leave MAC, where
he is a vice president.

Smeloff said he expects that if
agreement on salary and other
working conditions can be reached,
Ward will transfer to the SMUD payroll "fairly soon," well before next
summer's scheduled plant startup.

Last week, the board's Audit Committee chaired by Smeloff, decided,
having Ward on staff would avoid potential conflicts-of-interest result in
smoother administration and save
MAC's high overhead charges.

SMUD during part of the plant's original construction.

The committee expressed concern
last month there might be a conflict
between Jackson's position as a MAC
employee and his role as part of the
review group.

Smeloff said SMUD might want to
ask the review group about MAC's
performance or get advice on how to
change the contract with MAC.

The Rancho Seco plant - responsible for producing about half of
SMUD's power - has been out of
service since an over-cooling incident Dec. 26, 1985.

SMUD
SACRAMENTO MUNICIPAL UTILITY DISTRICT □ P. O. Box 15830, Sacramento CA 95852-1830, (916) 452-3211
AN ELECTRIC SYSTEM SERVING THE HEART OF CALIFORNIA

MAY 26 9 35 AM '87

May 21, 1987


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: SMUD Contract No. B865 with MAC
     FPPC File No. I-87-122

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

     Cortus Koehler handed me your letter of May 5. I
enclose a copy of my letter of May 15 to Diane Griffiths.
Probably you have seen it already. As indicated in that
letter, the District will be proceeding with its audit of the
Management Analysis Company contract. I understand from Ms.
Griffiths that it will be in order for Arthur Andersen and Co.,
our auditor, to consult with your office on legal questions
relating to Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act of 1974
and the related code sections and regulations. We certainly
appreciate the cooperation you have been giving us.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        David S. Kaplan
                                        General Counsel

Attachment

cc: Cortus Koehler


DISTRICT HEADQUARTERS □ 6201 S Street, Sacramento CA 95817-1899

State of California

Memorandum

To : Bob Leidigh
    Kathy Donovan
    John McClean

Date : May 21, 1987

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Diane Griffiths

Subject : S.M.U.D.

Please read the attached letter. No comments required.

SMUD
SACRAMENTO MUNICIPAL UTILITY DISTRICT □ P. O. Box 15830, Sacramento CA 95852-1830, (916) 452-3211
AN ELECTRIC SYSTEM SERVING THE HEART OF CALIFORNIA

MAY 13 2 05 AM '87

May 15, 1987


Diane Griffiths
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: SMUD Contract No. B865 with MAC Corporation

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation of a week or so ago.

Management Analysis Company (MAC) has been supplying SMUD with a number of key executives to assist in the Rancho Seco restart effort. The District has decided that it would be well to have our outside auditor, Arthur Andersen & Co., audit the contract to make sure that it is being administered in compliance with the requirements of Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act of 1974 and the related code sections and regulations. To make it clear that the audit is truly independent, we have advised the Arthur Andersen people that they should feel free to consult with your office on any legal questions that may arise. I have, of course, supplied them with copies of the pertinent sections of the Political Reform Act of 1974 and with copies of Section 18700 through 18704 of

Diane Griffiths                -2-                May 15, 1987


the FPPC Regulations.  I understand from our previous con-

versation that it is in order for your office to assist us

in this manner.

     I want to thank you for your cooperation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        David S. Kaplan
                                        General Counsel

cc:  Arthur Andersen & Co.
     Attn: Rick Blumenfeld

Koehler Vice-President Board of Directors This is a letter from the California Fair Political Practices Commission dated April 21, 1987, addressed to Cortus T. Koehler, Vice-President of the Board of Directors for the Sacramento Municipal Utility District. The letter informs Mr. Koehler that his request for advice under the Political Reform Act was received on April 20, 1987, and advises him to contact Robert E. Adm. Code Sec. 18329) is referenced, and it is noted that both the letter and response may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. The letter concludes with "Very truly yours," signed by Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel, and includes the initials DMG:plh at the bottom.
